faqs.question: How fast can you get to my home?
faqs.answer: For {{city}} and surrounding areas, {{businessName}} typically arrives within {{responseTime}}. For emergencies like burst pipes or major leaks, we prioritize dispatch and aim to be there within 1 hour.

faqs.question: Do you charge for estimates?
faqs.answer: Estimates for larger jobs like repiping, water heater replacement, and sewer work are always free. For service calls, we charge a diagnostic fee that gets applied toward the repair cost if you choose to proceed with us.

faqs.question: Are your plumbers licensed and insured?
faqs.answer: Yes. {{businessName}} is fully licensed ({{licenseNumber}}) and insured. Every plumber on our team holds a valid license, has passed a background check, and receives ongoing training to stay current with codes and best practices.

faqs.question: Do you guarantee your work?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. All repairs come with a {{warrantyYears}}-year labor warranty. If anything we fixed fails within that period, we will come back and fix it again at no charge. Parts carry their own manufacturer warranties on top of that.

faqs.question: Why does my drain keep clogging even after I clear it?
faqs.answer: Recurring clogs usually indicate a deeper problem such as grease buildup in the line, tree root intrusion, or a bellied (sagging) pipe section. A video camera inspection can identify the root cause so we can fix it permanently rather than just clearing the symptom.

faqs.question: Are chemical drain cleaners safe to use?
faqs.answer: We do not recommend them. Chemical drain cleaners contain caustic ingredients that can corrode pipes over time, especially older metal or PVC plumbing. They also tend to only punch a small hole through the clog rather than clearing it fully. Enzyme-based cleaners are a safer alternative for maintenance, but stubborn clogs should be handled professionally.

faqs.question: What is hydro jetting and when is it needed?
faqs.answer: Hydro jetting uses a high-pressure water stream to scour the inside of your pipes, removing grease buildup, mineral deposits, and even tree roots. It is the most thorough drain cleaning method available and is especially effective for recurring clogs or main sewer line issues. Call {{businessName}} at {{phone}} to find out if hydro jetting is right for your situation.

faqs.question: How long should my water heater last?
faqs.answer: Traditional tank water heaters typically last 8-12 years, while tankless models can last 20 years or more with proper maintenance. If your tank heater is over 10 years old and showing signs of rust, leaking, or inconsistent heating, it is usually more cost-effective to replace rather than repair.

faqs.question: Should I switch to a tankless water heater?
faqs.answer: Tankless heaters cost more upfront but provide endless hot water, use 24-34% less energy, and last nearly twice as long. They are especially beneficial for larger families or homes with multiple bathrooms. {{businessName}} offers free consultations to help you compare the options for your specific home and usage.

faqs.question: Why is my water heater making popping or rumbling noises?
faqs.answer: Those sounds are almost always caused by sediment buildup at the bottom of the tank. As the burner heats water trapped beneath the sediment layer, it creates steam bubbles that pop. Annual flushing can resolve this. If the noise is severe or the unit is old, it may be time for a replacement.

faqs.question: My water heater is leaking from the bottom. Can it be repaired?
faqs.answer: A leak from the bottom of a tank water heater usually means the inner tank has corroded through, which cannot be repaired. This is a sign the unit needs replacement. Turn off the power and water supply and call {{businessName}} at {{phone}} for same-day water heater replacement service.

faqs.question: Do you offer 24/7 emergency service?
faqs.answer: Yes. {{businessName}} has licensed plumbers on call around the clock, including weekends and holidays. Our trucks are fully stocked so we can handle most emergencies in a single visit. Call {{phone}} any time, day or night.

faqs.question: A pipe burst in my home. What should I do right now?
faqs.answer: First, shut off your main water valve immediately to stop the flow. Then turn off electricity to the affected area if water is near outlets or appliances. Move valuables away from the water. Then call {{businessName}} at {{phone}} for emergency response. We will walk you through additional steps over the phone while a plumber is dispatched.

faqs.question: Is there an extra charge for after-hours or weekend service?
faqs.answer: We keep our emergency rates fair and transparent. While after-hours calls may carry a modest service fee, we always provide an upfront quote before starting work. There are no hidden charges or surprise bills with {{businessName}}.
